title: Low Income/Subsidized Private Rental Housing * Veteran/Military Personnel Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Housing & Shelter > Low Income/Subsidized Private Rental Housing * Veteran/Military Personnel Issues 2 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions Agricultural Laborers Foster Families Mental Health Issues Older Adults Substance Use Disorder Issues Veteran/Military Personnel Issues Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CENTRAL OREGON VETERANS OUTREACH COVO LOW-INCOME HOUSING FOR VETERANS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Housing units (a mix of single family homes and multi-family housing located in Bend, Oregon) are prioritized for homeless, low-income veterans. COVO also assists homeless, low-income veterans with housing search for rentals throughout Central, Oregon. 61510 S Highway 97 Suite 100 Bend, OR 977023706 Eligibility: Homeless Veterans who income-qualify and pass property management screening for tenants Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm Email (541) 383-2793 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY VETERAN SERVICES OFFICE VETERANS HOUSING PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides affordable rental housing and case-managed transitional housing for military veterans and their families transitioning back to civilian life. 1870 W 11th Avenue Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Veterans with a discharge other than dishonorable or General Court Martial, experiencing homelessness and those at risk of becoming homeless, and under 80% Lane County Average Median Income Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (458) 221-0718 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTRAL OREGON VETERANS OUTREACH COVO LOW-INCOME HOUSING FOR VETERANS ST VINCENT DE PAUL OF LANE COUNTY VETERANS HOUSING PROJECT
